# VER-QUIC-CRT-0023 - QUIC CRT server chain-policy callback path verification

## Scope

Verify server-side CertificateChainPolicy delegation on the existing callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path and its regression boundaries without widening broader policy promises.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0125

## Verification Method

Execution, inspection, and regression evidence.

## Preconditions

- The Incursa.Quic library builds successfully on net10.0.
- The callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired floor from REQ-QUIC-CRT-0124 remains in place.
- The server runtime/bridge delegates CertificateChainPolicy-driven client-certificate evaluation to BCL X509Chain primitives before invoking the existing callback seam.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the focused server chain-policy requirement-home tests and the existing callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired tests.
- Run the current CRT, API, and INT guards that must remain unchanged, especially the mainstream client TLS and exact-pinning floors plus the transfer/retry contracts.
- Run the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep.
- Run the repo-local SpecTrace validation and markdown rendering checks after the canonical JSON updates.

## Expected Result

- The server callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path now honors CertificateChainPolicy through BCL X509Chain delegation.
- Trust/policy mismatch fails closed absent callback override.
- Standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode remains rejected.
- Mainstream client TLS, exact pinning, transfer, retry, 0-RTT, and key update posture remain unchanged.

## Status

This verification artifact covers the narrow server-side CertificateChainPolicy follow-on on the callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path while leaving standalone CertificateRevocationCheckMode deferred.

## Related Artifacts

- SPEC-QUIC-CRT
- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0023
- WI-QUIC-CRT-0023

## Acceptance Criteria

- The server callback-driven ClientCertificateRequired path now accepts CertificateChainPolicy and passes a built X509Chain plus computed SslPolicyErrors into the existing callback.
- A matching custom trust policy succeeds on the real managed path, and a trust/policy mismatch fails closed when the callback does not override acceptance.
- The existing callback-driven floor, missing-client-certificate failure, mainstream client TLS mode, exact pinning floor, transfer contract, retry contract, and standalone revocation-mode rejection remain unchanged.
